seo.title: Flooring in Spokane | Hardwood, LVP, Tile & Laminate | Cabinets Plus
seo.description: Hardwood, luxury vinyl plank, tile, and laminate flooring in Spokane. Compare hundreds of samples at our Sprague Avenue showroom alongside the cabinets and counters in your remodel.

blocks.heading: Hardwood, Vinyl, Tile & Carpet in Spokane
blocks.subtext: A full range of flooring from trusted manufacturers, all in one Spokane showroom — with the selection, pricing, and local know-how to help you pick the right fit.

blocks.title: Why Buy Flooring at Cabinets Plus
blocks.paragraphs: There are plenty of flooring stores in Spokane, but our customers keep coming back for three simple reasons. **Selection** — we stock a wider range of hardwood, LVP, SPC, laminate, tile, and carpet than most local shops, so you can compare materials side by side instead of guessing. **Pricing** — because we work directly with trusted manufacturers, we keep prices honest without cutting corners on quality. And **local trust** — we've built our reputation in Spokane the slow way, one happy customer at a time, and we treat every project like it matters. Stop by the showroom and see the difference for yourself.

blocks.title: How We Help You Get the Right Floors

steps.title: See & Sample
steps.description: Walk our Spokane showroom, feel each material under your shoes, and take samples home to check them in your own light next to your cabinets and paint colors.

steps.title: Get Recommendations
steps.description: Tell us about the room, the traffic, and your budget. We'll point you to materials that fit — and steer you away from the ones that won't.

steps.title: Measure & Order
steps.description: Bring us your room measurements, or we'll help you figure out how to measure properly. Once you've picked your flooring, we'll order it and let you know when it's in.

steps.title: Pick Up Your Order
steps.description: We don't offer flooring installation, but we can recommend trusted local installers we've worked with for years. Or, if you're handy, plenty of LVP, SPC, and laminate options are designed for DIY install.

blocks.title: Choosing the Right Flooring for Your Spokane Home
blocks.paragraphs:
The right floor isn't about picking the prettiest plank — it's about matching the material to the room and the way you actually live. A floor that's perfect in a quiet bedroom might be a disaster in a kitchen full of kids and dogs, and a hardwood that looks beautiful in a dining room won't survive a week in a basement laundry room. Before anything else, think about how the space gets used: foot traffic, moisture, sunlight, pets, the works.

For high-traffic and wet areas — kitchens, mudrooms, bathrooms, basements — luxury vinyl plank (LVP) and SPC are tough to beat. They're fully waterproof, scratch-resistant, and tough enough for big dogs and Spokane winters, while still looking like real wood. Tile holds up just as well in bathrooms and entryways, and lasts for decades with almost no maintenance. For living rooms, dining rooms, and bedrooms, hardwood is still the gold standard — timeless, refinishable, and a real boost to resale value, especially in older Spokane craftsman and ranch homes.

Laminate is the budget-friendly pick for bedrooms and lower-traffic spaces, and carpet still wins where you want softness, warmth, and quiet underfoot. Whatever direction you lean, our team will give you straight, room-by-room recommendations based on what we've seen work in Spokane homes — no pressure, no upsells, just honest help finding the right fit.

blocks.heading: Step Into Something New
blocks.subtext: From rich hardwood and waterproof LVP to cozy carpet and durable tile, Cabinets Plus has the right flooring to match your style, your space, and the way you live in Spokane.

faqs.question: What's the best flooring for kitchens and bathrooms in Spokane?
faqs.answer: For wet areas, we typically recommend luxury vinyl plank (LVP), SPC, or tile. They're fully waterproof, easy to clean, and stand up to the daily abuse kitchens and bathrooms see. Hardwood and laminate aren't ideal where standing water is a risk.

faqs.question: How much does flooring cost in Spokane?
faqs.answer: For materials only, expect roughly $2–$5 per square foot for laminate and basic LVP, $4–$8 for premium LVP and SPC, $6–$12 for hardwood, and $3–$10 for tile depending on size and pattern. Carpet typically runs $2–$5 per square foot. Installation is separate and varies by installer.

faqs.question: What's the difference between LVP and SPC flooring?
faqs.answer: Both are vinyl plank products. LVP (luxury vinyl plank) has a flexible PVC core, while SPC (stone plastic composite) has a rigid mineral-based core that's harder, more dent-resistant, and better for uneven subfloors. SPC tends to be the more durable choice for heavy traffic.

faqs.question: Do you install flooring?
faqs.answer: We don't currently offer flooring installation — we focus on cabinet and countertop installation. But we can point you to trusted local installers we've worked with for years, or help you find DIY-friendly products if you want to handle it yourself.

faqs.question: Can you recommend a flooring installer?
faqs.answer: Yes. We've built relationships with reliable installers across Spokane County and North Idaho who've handled jobs for our customers for years. Ask us when you visit the showroom and we'll connect you.

faqs.question: Do I need to acclimate flooring before installation?
faqs.answer: Yes — hardwood, laminate, and even some LVP products need to sit in the home for 24–72 hours before install so they adjust to indoor humidity. Skipping this step is one of the top causes of gaps, buckling, and warranty claims. Any reputable installer will plan for this.

faqs.question: Can I install flooring over my existing floor?
faqs.answer: Sometimes. LVP, SPC, and laminate can often be floated over existing tile or vinyl if the surface is flat and stable. Hardwood and tile usually require the old floor to come out. Your installer will evaluate the subfloor before starting.

faqs.question: Do you sell flooring outside of Spokane?
faqs.answer: Yes. We supply flooring to homeowners, contractors, and house flippers throughout Spokane County and North Idaho — Spokane Valley, Liberty Lake, Cheney, Airway Heights, Medical Lake, Deer Park, Coeur d'Alene, Post Falls, and Hayden.
